stories.lens: The confirmation of a divorce involving two well-known television actors on a prime-time reality show is the kind of moment that drives massive engagement among Indian TV audiences who follow both personalities closely.
stories.article:
Actor Akanksha Chamola has confirmed her divorce from Anupamaa star Gaurav Khanna while appearing on Lock Upp Season 2, bringing one of Indian television's more closely watched personal stories into definitive public view. The admission, made within the confines of the reality show's format, was one of the more significant personal revelations to emerge from the programme's current season.

Lock Upp, which puts celebrity contestants in a jail-themed setting and encourages them to be open about their lives under pressure from the host and fellow contestants, has become a platform for such disclosures. Chamola's confirmation of the divorce ends months of speculation about the couple's relationship status, which had been the subject of considerable discussion among followers of the Indian television industry.

Gaurav Khanna rose to significant prominence through his portrayal of Anuj Kapadia in the long-running Star Plus serial Anupamaa, which made him one of the more recognisable faces of prime-time Hindi television. Chamola is also a familiar name in the TV industry. The breakdown of their marriage, now confirmed on national television, is likely to generate sustained discussion across entertainment media and fan communities.

The revelation underlines the growing role of reality television in India as a space where personal narratives — sometimes painful ones — become entertainment. Whether by design or circumstance, Lock Upp Season 2 has once again demonstrated its ability to generate headline-worthy moments.
key_facts: Akanksha Chamola confirms divorce from Gaurav Khanna on Lock Upp Season 2
Gaurav Khanna is known for playing Anuj Kapadia in Anupamaa
Revelation made on Kangana Ranaut's reality show

stories.headline: Suniel Shetty reveals his 15-month-old granddaughter offers laddoos to PM Modi's photo every day
stories.what: Actor Suniel Shetty, currently in the spotlight with the release of Welcome to the Jungle, has shared a charming anecdote about his family life: his 15-month-old granddaughter apparently offers laddoos to a photograph of Prime Minister Narendra Modi on a daily basis. The light-hearted revelation highlights the actor's family dynamics and has been widely shared for its endearing quality. The story comes as Shetty enjoys renewed visibility thanks to his prominent role in the new release.
stories.lens: A beloved actor's personal vignette about his granddaughter becomes a feel-good human interest moment that connects entertainment with everyday family life for Indian readers.
stories.article:
Suniel Shetty, one of Bollywood's most enduring stars and currently appearing in the ensemble comedy Welcome to the Jungle, has shared a delightful anecdote that has quickly warmed social media: his 15-month-old granddaughter offers laddoos to a photograph of Prime Minister Narendra Modi every single day.

The actor, speaking publicly about his family life, described the daily ritual with evident affection. The image of a toddler making such an offering — apparently as a matter of habitual routine — has resonated widely, blending the worlds of celebrity, family, and patriotic sentiment in a manner that feels entirely unforced.

Shetty has been a prominent presence in entertainment news this week, given the release of Welcome to the Jungle, in which he stars alongside Akshay Kumar. The film's strong opening day performance has put both actors back in the commercial spotlight, and Shetty's personal charm — evident in this kind of candid family disclosure — continues to endear him to audiences well beyond his action-hero heyday.

The anecdote, while light, speaks to the actor's grounded family life and the affection he clearly holds for his grandchild. It is the kind of story that travels far beyond film circles and connects with everyday Indian audiences who find in it something recognisably warm and human.
key_facts: Suniel Shetty's granddaughter is 15 months old
She reportedly offers laddoos to PM Modi's photo daily
Shetty shared the anecdote amid Welcome to the Jungle's release
stories.image_credit: Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports / Wikimedia Commons

stories.headline: Director Neeraj Ghaywan slams Tere Ishk Mein's violence as 'not even smart, it's plain ugly'
stories.what: Filmmaker Neeraj Ghaywan, known for critically acclaimed work like Masaan, has publicly criticised the violence depicted in the web series Tere Ishk Mein, calling it 'not even smart' and 'plain ugly'. The pointed remarks from a respected auteur add artistic and ethical weight to what might otherwise remain an audience debate. Ghaywan's intervention is likely to draw fresh scrutiny to the show's content choices and the broader conversation about how violence is depicted in Indian OTT productions.
stories.lens: When a filmmaker of Ghaywan's standing calls out a mainstream OTT production's violence publicly, it shifts what could be a niche content debate into a mainstream conversation about craft and responsibility.
stories.article:
Filmmaker Neeraj Ghaywan has launched a sharp public critique of the violence in Tere Ishk Mein, the web series currently streaming on an OTT platform, describing it as 'not even smart' and 'plain ugly'. The directness of the rebuke — coming from a director celebrated for his nuanced, humanist filmmaking — has made it one of the more discussed film-world interventions of the day.

Ghaywan did not frame his criticism as mere squeamishness about on-screen violence, but rather as an aesthetic and moral objection to how that violence is constructed and presented. By distinguishing between violence that serves a narrative purpose and violence that is gratuitous or technically inept, he set a critical benchmark that puts the onus back on the makers of Tere Ishk Mein to justify their choices.

The criticism arrives at a moment when Indian OTT platforms have been under increasing scrutiny for graphic content. Streaming services have significantly raised the intensity of violence, language, and sexual content in their productions over recent years, often in pursuit of engagement metrics. Ghaywan's comments add a filmmaker's voice to a conversation that has largely been dominated by regulators and audience complaints.

For the team behind Tere Ishk Mein, the remarks represent an unwanted spotlight. Whether they respond publicly or allow the work to speak for itself, Ghaywan's intervention has ensured the show's content choices will be examined more critically by both audiences and fellow filmmakers.
key_facts: Neeraj Ghaywan criticises Tere Ishk Mein's violence as 'plain ugly'
Ghaywan calls the violence 'not even smart'
Criticism reignites debate over graphic content on Indian OTT platforms

stories.headline: Welcome to the Jungle opens strong worldwide on Day 1, beats Bhooth Bangla and Cocktail 2
stories.what: Akshay Kumar's ensemble comedy Welcome to the Jungle has opened to a strong worldwide box office on its first day, surpassing the Day 1 collections of two major upcoming Bollywood releases — Bhooth Bangla and Cocktail 2. The film, which features a sprawling cast including Suniel Shetty, marks a significant opening for the franchise's latest instalment. The result positions Welcome to the Jungle as one of the stronger commercial openers of the season.
stories.lens: A solid Day 1 worldwide collection confirms audience appetite for big-screen Bollywood comedies and signals that the Welcome franchise still carries real commercial muscle.
stories.article:
Welcome to the Jungle arrived in cinemas with considerable commercial momentum, posting a worldwide Day 1 box office collection that outpaced the opening day numbers of two rival Bollywood productions — Bhooth Bangla and Cocktail 2. The Akshay Kumar-led comedy, part of the long-running Welcome franchise, appears to have benefited from strong brand recognition and a packed holiday calendar.

The film boasts an ensemble cast headlined by Akshay Kumar and Suniel Shetty, two actors whose combined star power has historically driven footfalls. Industry trackers noted that the worldwide Day 1 figure placed the film ahead of the anticipated openings for Bhooth Bangla and Cocktail 2, making it a talking point in trade circles as a benchmark for the ongoing season.

Day 2 collections were also being tracked closely, with The Times of India reporting that the film remained 'eye-catching' in its early run — suggesting the opening-day momentum was being sustained into the second day. Extended weekends and festival periods typically amplify such performances, giving the film a window to consolidate its early numbers.

For Akshay Kumar, whose recent box-office track record has been mixed, a healthy Welcome to the Jungle opening is commercially significant. The franchise has a loyal following built over multiple instalments, and a strong debut reaffirms its drawing power with mainstream multiplex audiences across India.
key_facts: Welcome to the Jungle beats Bhooth Bangla and Cocktail 2 on Day 1 worldwide
Film stars Akshay Kumar and Suniel Shetty
Day 2 collections described as 'eye-catching' by trade trackers

stories.headline: Baahubali was called 'Indian cinema's biggest disaster' on release day, reveals SS Rajamouli
stories.what: Director SS Rajamouli has revealed that Baahubali — now celebrated as one of Indian cinema's greatest blockbusters — was declared a catastrophic failure on the day of its release, with a deficit of ₹70 crore. The filmmaker disclosed this startling behind-the-scenes detail, underscoring how close the franchise came to being written off entirely. The revelation offers a rare and candid look at the high-stakes gamble that preceded one of Bollywood and Telugu cinema's most iconic success stories.
stories.lens: A ₹70 crore opening-day deficit on a film that went on to redefine Indian cinema is a reminder of how unpredictable — and unforgiving — the film industry can be, even for its greatest hits.
stories.article:
Baahubali, the magnum opus that redefined the scale of Indian filmmaking and became a global phenomenon, was branded 'Indian cinema's biggest disaster' on its very release day, director SS Rajamouli has revealed. The film, he said, had a deficit of ₹70 crore on day one — a jaw-dropping figure that suggests distributors and exhibitors had written it off almost immediately after its opening.

Rajamouli's admission is remarkable given what Baahubali ultimately became: a franchise that shattered box-office records across India and earned widespread international acclaim. The revelation underscores the brutal economics of big-budget filmmaking, where opening-day collections against advance bookings and distribution costs can make or break a film's perception within hours.

The director did not elaborate on how the tide turned, but the film's eventual trajectory is well-documented — Baahubali grew into a cultural juggernaut, with its sequel Baahubali: The Conclusion breaking records across the country. The question 'Why did Kattappa kill Baahubali?' became one of the most discussed pop-culture moments in Indian entertainment history.

Rajamouli's candid disclosure adds a new layer to the Baahubali legend — that the film's greatness was not immediately obvious even to those in the business. For aspiring filmmakers and industry insiders alike, it is a sobering and inspiring reminder that initial numbers do not always tell the full story.
key_facts: Baahubali had a deficit of ₹70 crore on its release day
SS Rajamouli revealed the film was called 'Indian cinema's biggest disaster'
Baahubali later became one of India's highest-grossing film franchises
stories.image_credit: Dani Charles Silverscreen Media Inc. (https://silverscreen.in) / Wikimedia Commons
